Apollo HS  ›  Project Home
Design Preview
ISD 742 School Modernization Initiative

Building Our Future

Explore the architectural designs and mock-ups for spaces across Apollo High School. Scan the QR codes posted in each space to see what it will look like after renovation.

Apex Athletics Center
The Apex Athletics Center will be a multi-purpose indoor athletic complex that will serve student-athletes from the St. Cloud Crush teams, Apollo High School teams, and St. Cloud Tech High School teams. The facility will also be available for community rentals when it is not being used by students from ISD 742.
Construction Starting→
Main Entrance (Door 1)
The new main entrance of Apollo High School will be located at the current Door 2 area and will become Door 1. As guests arrive, they will see a raised entrance that leads into the secured entry.
Planning→
Activities Entrance (Door 7)
The new Apollo Activities entrance will be located at Door 7. This space will create a fresh and welcoming look for one of the main entrances to the building.
Planning→
Exterior & Wayfinding
New wayfinding will guide visitors across campus and along the road as they approach the building. Clear signs will help guests find the areas they need with ease.
Planning→
Apollo Commons
The Apollo Commons will receive a fresh new look with a new ceiling, added wood features on the walls to brighten the space, and new wayfinding to help guide students, families, and guests to the Performing Arts wing. A new concession stand will also be added to better serve events and activities in the area. Completion will take place at the end of the summer of 2027.
Planning→
Kitchen & Serving Lines
The Apollo kitchen and serving lines will be renovated to create a larger space and reduce congestion during lunch. The updated design will allow the school to offer more lunch options for students. The renovation will expand into the space currently used for hallways, which will be removed to open the area.
Planning→
Fine Arts
The Fine Arts area will receive a full renovation of the band and choir rooms. The project will also add new storage for theater and fine arts to support student programs.
Planning→
Storm Shelter / 4th Gym
This new space meets current building code requirements for storm shelters. It will provide a safe, secure area for all students and staff during severe weather events.
Under Construction→
Technical Education Center
This is the new Technical Education Center. This space will expand learning opportunities for students through a Certified Nursing Assistant classroom, an Emergency Medical Technician classroom, an additional flexible classroom, and a makerspace where students can explore and work with new technology. The project also includes updates to current classrooms.
Under Construction→
Hallways & Corridors
Our updated main corridor hallways will feature new carpet and clear wayfinding signs for each wing of the building. These updates will create a clean, welcoming space and help students and visitors find their way with ease.
Planning→
North Resource Center
This area will be transformed into new classrooms and a new hallway within the existing North Resource Center. The updated space will include classrooms for computer science, culinary, and visual arts (photo, video, and yearbook), along with a flexible classroom for staff use.
Starting Soon→

🏆  Space Detail

Apex Athletics Center

New multi-purpose indoor athletic complex serving ISD 742 student-athletes and the community.

About This Space

The Apex Athletics Center will be a multi-purpose indoor athletic complex that will serve student-athletes from the St. Cloud Crush teams, Apollo High School teams, and St. Cloud Tech High School teams. The facility will also be available for community rentals when it is not being used by students from ISD 742.

The facility will feature a large indoor turf space designed to support year-round training and competition. Athletic programs that will benefit from the space include soccer, softball, baseball, football, lacrosse, golf, and track and field.

Construction is scheduled to begin in May 2026, with the project expected to be completed in Spring 2027.

This new facility will provide student-athletes with a safe and reliable space to train, build skills, and compete throughout the entire year. It will strengthen opportunities for both school programs and the broader community.

📱 Space QR Code

Post in Apex Athletics Center so visitors can scan to see the renovation design.

Construction Starting
School
Apollo

Renovation Design Preview — ISD 742
🏠  Space Detail

Main Entrance (Door 1)

New secure main entrance with constellation lighting, the relocated Apollo capsule, and updated main office.

About This Space

The new main entrance of Apollo High School will be located at the current Door 2 area and will become Door 1. As guests arrive, they will see a raised entrance that leads into the secured entry.

The Apollo capsule will move inside the building and guide visitors as they enter Door 1. Under the raised entrance, lighting will represent different constellations visible in Minnesota — giving teachers opportunities to connect this feature to classroom learning.

Guests will enter through this new secure entrance during the school day and will be buzzed into the main office. The updated front office will include a new waiting and seating area for visitors, creating a welcoming first stop for families while supporting a secure entry into the building.

A large monument sign reading "Apollo High School" will be clearly visible as guests approach the building.

Construction will begin in the summer of 2027 and will be completed in the summer of 2028.

🏈  Space Detail

Activities Entrance (Door 7)

Fresh new Apollo Activities entrance at Door 7 for families attending events and students arriving each morning.

About This Space

The new Apollo Activities entrance will be located at Door 7. This space will create a fresh and welcoming look for one of the main entrances to the building.

It will serve as an inviting entry for families attending evening activities and for students arriving each morning. The updated entrance will help create a strong first impression and reflect pride in our school.

This work will start Summer of 2028 and be completed Fall of 2028.

Exterior & Wayfinding

New campus wayfinding signs and exterior building staining for a clean, refreshed look.

About This Space

New wayfinding will guide visitors across campus and along the road as they approach the building. Clear signs will help guests find the areas they need with ease.

You may also notice a change in the building's color. The exterior will be stained in the summer of 2027, giving Apollo a clean and refreshed look.

🍽  Space Detail

Apollo Commons

Refreshed commons with new ceiling, wood features, wayfinding, concession stand, and an enclosed outdoor commons extension.

About This Space

The Apollo Commons will receive a fresh new look with a new ceiling, added wood features on the walls to brighten the space, and new wayfinding to help guide students, families, and guests to the Performing Arts wing. A new concession stand will also be added to better serve events and activities in the area. Completion will take place at the end of the summer of 2027.

The current outdoor commons will be enclosed and have a roof added, creating more seating for the cafeteria and providing a more open, updated commons space for students. Toward the back of the extended space, you can see the future locations of the new school store and A La Carte area — located where the Eagle graphic is shown.

This extended commons construction will begin in the summer of 2027 and will be completed in the summer of 2028.

🍳  Space Detail

Kitchen & Serving Lines

Renovated kitchen and expanded serving lines with a new central dishwashing area to improve lunch flow.

About This Space

The Apollo kitchen and serving lines will be renovated to create a larger space and reduce congestion during lunch. The updated design will allow the school to offer more lunch options for students. The renovation will expand into the space currently used for hallways, which will be removed to open the area.

Students will return their lunch trays to a new dishwashing area located in the center of the commons for easier access and flow. The image also shows the updated look of the gym entrance.

Construction will begin in June of 2028 and will be complete by the start of the 2028 school year.

🎶  Space Detail

Fine Arts

Full renovation of band and choir rooms with new practice rooms and added storage for theater and fine arts.

About This Space

The Fine Arts area will receive a full renovation of the band and choir rooms. The project will also add new storage for theater and fine arts to support student programs.

Lockers in the corridor will be removed to create space for new practice rooms. These updates will give students more room to learn, rehearse, and perform.

Construction will begin in June 2028 and will be complete before the start of school in September 2028.

🏅  Space Detail

Storm Shelter / 4th Gym

New building serving as a code-compliant storm shelter and fourth gym for PE, extracurriculars, and community use.

About This Space

This new space meets current building code requirements for storm shelters. It will provide a safe, secure area for all students and staff during severe weather events.

During the school day, it will also serve as a fourth gym, giving our students more space for physical education and activities. After school, the space will support extracurricular programs and be available for community use.

Construction of this space will start this August and be complete in June of 2027.

🔬  Space Detail

Technical Education Center

New CNA classroom, EMT classroom, makerspace, and flexible learning spaces to expand student opportunities.

About This Space

This is the new Technical Education Center. This space will expand learning opportunities for students through a Certified Nursing Assistant classroom, an Emergency Medical Technician classroom, an additional flexible classroom, and a makerspace where students can explore and work with new technology. The project also includes updates to current classrooms.

Construction is already underway, and the entire project will be completed in time for the start of the 2028 school year.

🚶  Space Detail

Hallways & Corridors

New carpet, wing wayfinding signs, and fire walls with wood-finish doors in the 400 and 600 wing corridors.

About This Space

Our updated main corridor hallways will feature new carpet and clear wayfinding signs for each wing of the building. These updates will create a clean, welcoming space and help students and visitors find their way with ease.

New fire walls will also be added to the corridors in the 400 and 600 wings. These walls will provide added fire protection during an emergency and are required as part of the new construction. The fire doors feature a wood finish, giving the hallways a fresh and clean look.

Construction will begin in the summer of 2027 and will be complete for the start of the 2027 school year.

📚  Space Detail

North Resource Center

New computer science, culinary, and visual arts classrooms built within the existing North Resource Center.

About This Space

This area will be transformed into new classrooms and a new hallway within the existing North Resource Center. The updated space will include classrooms for computer science, culinary, and visual arts (photo, video, and yearbook), along with a flexible classroom for staff use.

Construction will begin in December 2026 and will be completed in time for the start of the 2027 school year.
